description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

city and county seat of Spartanburg County, South Carolina, United States

city in Maryland, United States
description_wiki: Baltimore ( BAWL-tim-or, locally:  BAWL-dim-OR or  BAWL-mər), also known as Baltimore City, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Maryland.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Newport News () is an independent city in southeastern Virginia, United States.

description: largest city of Morocco
description_wiki: Casablanca (, US also ; Arabic: الدار البيضاء, romanized: ad-Dār al-Bayḍāʾ, lit. 'the White House', IPA: [adˈdaːr ɫbajdˤaːʔ]) is the largest city in Morocco and the country's economic and business centre.

surface_forms: Algiers, where I was at the seaport
description: capital city of Algeria
description_wiki: Algiers is the capital city of Algeria, located on the Mediterranean Sea in the north-central portion of the country.

description: capital city of Campania, Italy
description_wiki: Naples is the regional capital of Campania, Italy.

description: 1944 battle in Italy
description_wiki: The Battle of Anzio was a battle of the Italian Campaign of World War II that commenced 22 January 1944.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: capital and largest city of Italy
description_wiki: Rome is the capital city and most populated comune (municipality) of Italy.

description: formation of the United States Army (since 1917)
description_wiki: The 3rd Infantry Division (3ID) (nicknamed Rock of the Marne) is a combined arms division of the United States Army based at Fort Stewart, Georgia.

description: active United States Army National Guard formation
description_wiki: The 36th Infantry Division ("Arrowhead") also known as the "Panther Division", the "Lone Star Division", "The Texas Army", and the "T-patchers", is an infantry division of the U.S. Army and part of the Texas Army National Guard.

description: formation of the United States Army (1920-1968)
description_wiki: The 45th Infantry Division was an infantry division of the United States Army, most associated with the Oklahoma Army National Guard, from 1920 to 1968.

surface_forms: down to Salerno, which was south of Naples in Italy
description: Italian city and commune of Campania
description_wiki: Salerno (UK: , US: ; Italian: [saˈlɛrno] ; Neapolitan: Salierno [saˈljernə]) is an ancient city and comune (municipality) in Campania, southwestern Italy, and is the capital of the province of the same name.

description: Allied invasion of southern France on 15 August 1944
description_wiki: Operation Dragoon (initially Operation Anvil), known as Débarquement de Provence in French (lit. 'Provence Landing'), was the code name for the landing operation of the Allied invasion of Provence (Southern France) on 15 August 1944.

surface_forms: the pope declared Rome as an open city
description: pope of the Catholic Church from 1939 to 1958
description_wiki: Pope Pius XII (Italian: Pio XII; born Eugenio Maria Giuseppe Giovanni Pacelli; 2 March 1876 – 9 October 1958) was head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of Vatican City from 2 March 1939 until his death on 9 October 1958.
